article_title: B.C. Reg. 249/2025 – British Columbia Securities Commission

briefs.title: Investment fund advisers must now report fund costs in dollars per security to clients
briefs.summary: Starting January 1, 2026, BC securities rules require registered dealers and advisers (licensed investment professionals) to report investment fund charges to clients in a new way — showing the actual dollar cost of fund expenses per security held each day, not just as a percentage. Fund managers must supply this data to dealers and advisers so they can meet the new reporting requirements.
briefs.action_required: If you sell or advise on investment funds in BC, update your client reporting systems to show fund costs in dollars per security by January 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC NI 31-103 amended fund cost reporting rules take effect

briefs.article_title: B.C. Reg. 249/2025 – British Columbia Securities Commission
